mnemonics: VARiables Are Like BOXes (Variables store values like boxes hold items)
CONsole.log Is Your Debugging Best Friend (Always use it to print and check values)
Semi-Colons Signal The End (End statements with semicolons to avoid errors)
keyPoints: Use 'var', 'let', or 'const' to declare variables before using them
The console.log() function outputs information to the console for debugging
commonMistakes: Forgetting to declare a variable with var/let/const, causing a ReferenceError
Using the assignment operator (=) instead of the equality operator (== or ===) in conditionals
Missing semicolons at the end of statements, which can lead to unexpected behavior
